title: Phaseolus nelsonii
intro: Phaseolus nelsonii, also known as Desert Bean, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant and anti-diabetic activities. The bioactive constituents of Phaseolus nelsonii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being, particularly in traditional folk medicine practices.
taxonomy: Phaseolus nelsonii belongs to the family Fabaceae. This classification is due to the plant's characteristic pea-like flowers and fruit.
